name: Navaluenga Valley and Alberche River Loop
name_original: Valle Navaluenga y el Río Alberche— circular desde Navaluenga
description: Jog this 8.5-mile (13.7 km) moderate loop around Navaluenga, enjoying the Alberche River and historic bridge views.
region_intro: Jogging routes Navaluenga offer diverse terrain through the Alberche River valley, characterized by its riverine landscapes and surrounding hills. The area provides a mix of paths alongside water bodies and trails that ascend gently into the local countryside. Runners can expect routes that traverse open fields, pass through scattered woodlands, and offer views of the valley. Best jogging routes around Navaluenga The most popular jogging route is Navaluenga Valley and Alberche River Loop, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete, following the course of the Alberche River. Another top favourite among local runners is Navaluenga Valley loop from El Rincón de Gredos, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the valley, often completed in under an hour. Local runners also love the Navaluenga Valley loop from Navaluenga, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the valley's varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Navaluenga is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and open landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Navaluenga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 8 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Navaluenga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Navaluenga?
A: Navaluenga offers a good selection of running routes, with 15 distinct trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Navaluenga?
A: Yes, Navaluenga has routes suitable for beginners. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Estelar de Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.7 km long and offers a gentle introduction to the area's terrain.

Q: What are some options for long-distance running in Navaluenga?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14706995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Bridge – Old Bridge of Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 29 km trail. This route provides an extensive journey through the valley's varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Navaluenga?
A: Many of the running routes in Navaluenga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164699735/navaluenga-valley-and-alberche-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley and Alberche River Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14706719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley loop from El Rincón de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Navaluenga?
A: The running trails in Navaluenga offer diverse scenery, characterized by the Alberche River valley. You'll encounter paths alongside the river, open fields, scattered woodlands, and gentle ascents into the surrounding hills, providing views of the valley.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while running in Navaluenga?
A: Yes, the area features several points of interest. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4268792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt; (Old Bridge of Navaluenga) or enjoy views towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For natural sights, consider exploring areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4327965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de los Cangrejos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Navaluenga?
A: The running routes in Navaluenga are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.8 out of 5 stars from 8 reviews. Many runners appreciate the varied terrain and the scenic beauty of the Alberche River valley.

Q: Is Navaluenga suitable for family-friendly running or jogging with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the region offers an easy 4.7 km loop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Estelar de Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, which could be a good option for families looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Are there running routes in Navaluenga that are dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in natural settings like Navaluenga are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and check local regulations or specific route details for any restrictions, especially in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Navaluenga?
A: Navaluenga's climate, typical of the Alberche River valley, generally offers pleasant conditions for running in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer mornings and evenings can also be suitable, while winters might be cooler but still offer opportunities for running, especially on lower elevation trails.

Q: Are there any running routes with good viewpoints in Navaluenga?
A: Yes, the region's rolling hills and valley setting provide several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Estelar de Navaluenga loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes a viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there options for public transport access to the running routes in Navaluenga?
A: Navaluenga is a town in the province of Ávila, Spain. While specific public transport details for individual trailheads are not provided, the town itself is accessible by bus from larger cities like Madrid or Ávila. From the town center, many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga Valley loop from Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, are directly accessible.
tips: This old stone bridge is located in a beautiful setting in the town of Navaluenga. The bridge allows you to cross the Alberche River and divides the two bathing areas of the Navaluenga natural pools during the summer months. An ideal place to add to my route.
Beautiful views that I find when I reach this point. A natural viewpoint of the Alberche river that is undoubtedly worth visiting.

Following the Río Alberche and from the town of Navaluenga, this valley appears, on the south side we find the Sierra de Gredos and to the north, the Sierra de Panamera that offers this green and wide valley.

faqs.question: How challenging is this circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: This 13.7 km circular route is generally considered a moderate run. While it requires good fitness, its mostly paved sections make it suitable for all skill levels, offering an accessible experience for joggers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring mostly paved surfaces, which is ideal for jogging. You'll also encounter riverside paths, tracks through agricultural fields and meadows, and sections passing through pine forests and oak groves, especially as you move through the Valle del Alberche.

faqs.question: What are the main sights along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience picturesque views of the Alberche Valley and the Río Alberche. Key highlights include the historic Puente Viejo de Navaluenga, a significant 16th-century Romanesque bridge. You'll also enjoy a dedicated View of the Alberche River, and the expansive beauty of the Navaluenga Valley, with views towards the Sierra de Gredos.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Navaluenga. You can find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for routes in the area. Specific parking areas are usually available near the town center or close to the river.

faqs.question: Is this circular jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. It's a great way to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or other trail users, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The autumn months are particularly recommended for jogging this route, as the landscape transforms with vibrant colors. However, the trail is enjoyable year-round. In warmer months, the Río Alberche and its natural pools offer a refreshing option, but be mindful of midday heat.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to access and enjoy this circular jogging route around Navaluenga and the Alberche River. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: On average, it takes approximately 1 hour and 27 minutes to complete this 13.7 km circular jogging route. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You will find parts of the trail that coincide with the Barranco Cambronal, the Camino de Piedralaves, and the Puente Viejo.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or public restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is primarily natural, being a circular trail starting and ending in Navaluenga, you'll find various facilities within the village. Navaluenga offers cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms, making it convenient for pre- or post-jog refreshments and breaks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate jogging route, we recommend bringing appropriate running shoes, comfortable athletic wear, and a water bottle to stay hydrated. Depending on the weather, consider a light jacket or sun protection. A phone for navigation or emergencies is also advisable.
